deeperExplanation: Class attributes belong to the Class itself and are shared by all instances. Instance attributes belong to a specific instance (`self.attr`). Lookups search the instance `__dict__` first, then the class `__dict__`.
internalImplementation: CPython stores instance attributes in a dictionary `instance.__dict__`.

examples.explanation: Class attribute shared across all Dog instances.

performanceConsiderations.spaceComplexity: Use `__slots__` to eliminate instance `__dict__` memory overhead for millions of small instances.

debuggingWalkthrough.bugDescription: Forgetting self when accessing instance attributes.
